    Home › Compare › Airbus A380 vs Antonov An-225

    Airbus A380 vs Antonov An-225

    Both aircraft at true relative scale — against a 1.8 m person and a double-decker bus.

    Wingspan and overall height are drawn to true scale from the sourced figures on each aircraft page. Compare any other aircraft in the live tool →

    The numbers

    Side by side

    Airbus A380Antonov An-225DifferenceBigger
    Length72.72 m84 m11.28 mAntonov An-225 +16%
    Wingspan79.75 m88.40 m8.65 mAntonov An-225 +11%
    Height24.09 m18.10 m5.99 mAirbus A380 +33%
    MTOW575 t640 t65 tAntonov An-225 +11%
    Range14,800 km15,400 km600 kmAntonov An-225 +4%
    Max speed945 km/h850 km/h95 km/hAirbus A380 +11%
    The verdict

    Which is actually bigger?

    The two heaviest aircraft that ever flew, built for opposite reasons. The An-225 is longer and heavier and lifts more; the A380 has the greater wingspan, a genuine second deck, and has carried over 850 people at once. One was built a single time for a space programme. The other was built 251 times for a market that never arrived.

    The Antonov An-225 Mriya is the longer of the two, at 84.00 m against 72.72 m — a difference of 11.28 m. It also has the greater wingspan, 8.65 m wider, so it is the bigger aircraft on both measures.

    The largest passenger aircraft ever built. The largest aircraft ever built. The silhouettes above are drawn at true relative scale from the sourced figures on each aircraft's page — same metres, same pixels, no artistic licence.

    The two are built for different jobs, which is why a straight capacity comparison does not apply.

    Airbus A380

    Airbus · Out of production (2021) — in service

    Full specification →

    Antonov An-225 Mriya

    Antonov · Destroyed (2022) — one built

    Full specification →

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
